text: Handling deadlines successfully is a crucial skill. Good time management starts with breaking large tasks into smaller, manageable steps. Prioritization helps you decide which tasks are most important, so you focus on what truly needs to be done first. It is also wise to be realistic about your abilities. Scope management means not taking on more work than you can handle. If a project becomes too large, you should discuss this with your team or manager. Finally, communication is key. If you see a problem, let others know early, rather than missing a deadline. With these strategies, you can reduce stress and meet your goals.
